name: Casco
description_short: Casco Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving Cumberland & York County with checking, savings, loans, and investment services.
description_long:
Casco Federal Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union operating in the Cumberland and York County regions of Maine. As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution, it operates under the NCUA regulatory framework and emphasizes community-based financial services with a "People Helping People" philosophy.

Casco FCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. On the lending side, they provide auto loans with promotional rates as low as 4.75% APR for up to 60 months, personal loans, home loans, student loans, and business loans & lines of credit. They also issue Visa credit cards with rewards programs and no annual fees. On the deposit side, they offer checking accounts, savings accounts, youth accounts, share certificates, and investment services. Their Account Services portal includes online banking, mobile banking, bill pay, e-statements, shared branching access, free ATM networks, and debit cards.

What distinguishes Casco FCU is their recent partnership with Eko to launch "Casco INVEST," a digital investment platform integrated directly into their online and mobile banking. This platform allows members to start investing with as little as $10 and choose between guided investing (professionally managed portfolios), self-directed investing (commission-free trading of 5,000+ stocks, bonds, and ETFs), or a hybrid approach. This positions them as one of the few traditional credit unions offering accessible, low-minimum digital investing.

As a regional credit union, Casco FCU's primary limitation is geographic—membership is restricted to those in Cumberland and York County. Their website content focuses on products and services rather than detailed terms, rates, or eligibility criteria, which may require direct member inquiry. The institution demonstrates active community engagement through seasonal closures, member benefits programs, and financial wellness content, though specific membership eligibility requirements are not detailed online.
services: Business loans and lines of credit
Casco INVEST (digital investment platform with managed and self-directed options)
Bill pay, e-statements, shared branching, free ATM access
pros: Auto loan rates as low as 4.75% APR with fast approvals (up to 60 months)
Visa credit card with no annual fee and rewards program
Casco INVEST platform allows investment account opening with as little as $10
Three investment options: guided (professionally managed), self-directed (5,000+ commission-free trades), or hybrid
Free ATM access through shared branching network
Integrated digital banking with online and mobile platforms including bill pay and e-statements
Not-for-profit, member-owned structure with emphasis on member benefits
cons: Geographic restriction to Cumberland and York County membership only
Limited rate and fee transparency on website; specific terms require direct inquiry
Relatively small regional institution compared to national credit unions or banks
No apparent mention of alternative products like payday alternative loans (PALs) despite NCUA authority to offer them
Investment minimums ($10) and fee structure not detailed on website
best_for: Local residents of Cumberland and York County seeking accessible investing with low minimums
Members prioritizing auto loans at competitive promotional rates with fast approval
Credit union members wanting integrated investment services alongside traditional banking
diagnosis: Casco FCU is ideal for Cumberland and York County residents seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with modern digital services, particularly those interested in low-minimum investing. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership limitation, making them inaccessible to those outside their service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
